description: Constitutional invariant registry (UTV2-1088 / INIT-1.3.1). Every constitutional invariant is a registry entry with a permanent ID. IDs are never reused. Registry changes require Governance Authority approval.

invariants.title: main is shipped truth
invariants.description: GitHub main branch is the authoritative source of shipped code and merge SHAs. Agent claims, chat summaries, and memory are never authoritative over the branch state.

invariants.title: No lane without preflight; no Done without truth-check
invariants.description: A lane must not start without a valid preflight token. A lane must not be marked Done without ops:truth-check passing. Both checks are mandatory and cannot be bypassed.

invariants.title: One issue → one lane → one branch → one PR
invariants.description: Each Linear issue maps to exactly one lane, one git branch, and one PR. Multiple issues must not share a lane. A lane must not produce multiple PRs for the same issue.

invariants.title: Proof must tie to merge SHA
invariants.description: A proof bundle is only valid when its SHA is bound to the merge SHA of the PR (the SHA after merge to main). Proof tied to a branch HEAD or pre-merge SHA is invalid and must not be accepted.

invariants.title: Tier label required before Ready
invariants.description: A Linear issue must carry a tier label (tier:T1, tier:T2, or tier:T3) before it can enter a Ready state. Untired issues must not be dispatched.

invariants.title: Lane manifest is sole authority for active lane state
invariants.description: docs/06_status/lanes/<issue>.json is the only authoritative record of lane state. Chat summaries, agent claims, and memory are not authoritative. The manifest is updated by kernel tooling only.

invariants.title: Domain package is pure — no I/O
invariants.description: @unit-talk/domain is a pure computation package. It must not perform I/O, access a database, make HTTP requests, or read environment variables. Domain purity is a hard boundary.

invariants.title: Dependency boundary: packages do not import from apps
invariants.description: Packages must never import from apps. Apps must never import from other apps. This prevents circular dependencies and keeps app-level side effects isolated. Enforced by ESLint import boundary rules.

invariants.title: Postgres outbox is the only delivery queue
invariants.description: All pick delivery attempts must flow through the Postgres outbox (distribution_outbox table). Exactly one DeliveryOutcome is recorded per attempt. No in-memory queue, no direct delivery bypass, no duplicate outcome writes.

invariants.title: Fail closed — no silent fallback to qualified/pass/done
invariants.description: When a transition cannot be completed or a condition cannot be verified, the system must fail closed. Silent fallback to 'qualified', 'pass', or 'done' states is prohibited. Ambiguity resolves to rejection, not acceptance.

invariants.title: Mechanical enforcement required for all invariants
invariants.description: If a constitutional invariant can be enforced mechanically (CI, DB constraint, trigger, RPC guard), it must be. An invariant that lives only in prose documentation is not constitutionally enforced. Each registry entry must specify at least one non-governance enforcing layer.

invariants.title: Pick writer authority: field-level ownership
invariants.description: Each pick field has a designated writer role (submitter, promoter, poster, settler, operator_override). Only the designated writer may update a field. Cross-role field writes are rejected at the RPC layer.

invariants.title: No truth-surface migration without tested rollback
invariants.description: Every SQL migration that modifies the truth surface (picks, market_universe, settlement, audit_log) must carry an executable down script in db/migrations-rollback/. The migration reversibility gate must pass before the PR can merge.

invariants.title: Audit log is append-only and immutable
invariants.description: The audit_log table is append-only. Delete and update operations on audit_log are rejected by a Postgres trigger. No retention policy may prune audit_log rows. Immutability is enforced at the database layer.

invariants.title: Pick lifecycle transitions are immutable once terminal
invariants.description: Once a pick reaches a terminal state (settled, voided), no further lifecycle transitions are permitted. The terminal state is permanent. Retroactive state changes to terminal picks are rejected.
